stories.title: Add pymysql to requirements-server.txt (I-022, Spool Story A)
stories.intent: I-022: scripts/report.py rewrites async DATABASE_URL to sync via _toSyncDriverUrl() (line 95) using mysql+pymysql://. SQLAlchemy then needs pymysql Python package to drive MariaDB synchronously. pymysql is NOT declared in requirements-server.txt -- only aiomysql>=0.2.0 is. EVERY CLI report path (--drive / --trends / --calibrate) crashes with ModuleNotFoundError on a clean venv. Mike has been doing all MariaDB ad-hoc work via the mysql binary CLI (separate from pymysql Python library), so the gap stayed silent. Fix: add pymysql>=1.1.0 to requirements-server.txt. Same dependency-declaration anti-pattern class as historical npm/pip 'works on my machine' gaps.

Fix surface: one dependency line + a NEW regression test. Verification: `pytest tests/server/test_requirements_pymysql_declared.py -v` -> 5 passed; `pytest tests/ -m 'not slow' -q` -> exit 0, zero regressions vs the 4149 baseline; `ruff check tests/server/test_requirements_pymysql_declared.py` -> clean; `python offices/pm/scripts/sprint_lint.py` -> 0 errors / 0 warnings across 5 stories. stories.title: Remove phantom sqlite fallback in report.py (I-023)
stories.intent: I-023: scripts/report.py:89 defines _DEFAULT_DB_URL_FALLBACK pointing at data/server_crawl.db which exists but has empty schema. Any CLI command using the fallback (when DATABASE_URL unset + --db-url omitted) crashes with sqlite3.OperationalError: no such table: drive_summary. Production never hits this (DATABASE_URL is set) but it's a confusing footgun for ad-hoc testing. Per CIO 2026-05-11 implicit Option A (PM + Spool joint recommendation): remove the fallback entirely; make --db-url or DATABASE_URL required. Cleaner failure mode; no phantom.

stories.title: Pi realtime_data NULL-drive_id orphan cleanup (B-072)
stories.intent: B-072: Spool 2026-05-11 audit found 61,293 NULL-drive_id rows in Pi-side realtime_data accumulated since Session 10's cleanup. Sources: reconnect-loop polling (engine-off), I-019 DriveDetector warm-restart gap, pre-DriveDetector grace period. Not poison (Spool's tuning filters on drive_id IS NOT NULL) but wasteful + grows unbounded. Approach 1 (recommended): periodic cleanup task via systemd timer; deletes NULL-drive_id rows older than 24h. Idempotent + logged.

stories.title: BT reconnect exponential backoff when OBDLink absent (I-025)
stories.intent: I-025: eclipse-obd reconnect loop fires every 30-60s FOREVER when OBDLink unreachable. Pi 5 combo chip (WiFi+BT shared radio) + Pi 5 default wifi.powersave=ON + continuous BT activity = WiFi drops (observed twice 2026-05-11). OS-side mitigation already applied via /etc/NetworkManager/conf.d/disable-wifi-powersave.conf (Fix #1, Pi 5 best practice). This story is Fix #2 -- reduce BT activity at the source via exponential backoff in runReconnectHeartbeat. Formula: min(30 * 2^min(failures,5), 900) seconds; cap at 15 min between attempts; reset on successful connect.

filesActuallyTouched: src/pi/obdii/reconnect_loop.py (UPDATE -- runReconnectHeartbeat gained exponential backoff. NEW module constants MAX_BACKOFF_SEC=900.0 + BACKOFF_EXP_CAP=5 (both added to __all__). NEW helper _nextAttemptInterval(consecutiveFailures, baseSec) -> min(baseSec * 2 ** min(consecutiveFailures, BACKOFF_EXP_CAP), MAX_BACKOFF_SEC); consecutiveFailures is the count BEFORE the current tick, so the FIRST failed-tick sleep == baseSec (V0.27.1 short-outage back-compat) -- this is also why tests/pi/integration/test_engine_on_late_adapter_e2e.py's `sleepDurations == [10.0]` assertion and tests/pi/obdii/test_connect_thread_safety.py both stay GREEN untouched. runReconnectHeartbeat: added a `consecutiveFailures` loop-local (0 initially), computes `backoffSec = _nextAttemptInterval(consecutiveFailures, tickIntervalSec)` once per tick before the connect attempt, uses it for the post-tick sleep on every non-success outcome AND on the already_in_flight skip (an in-flight skip is NOT a failure -- the counter holds, cadence stays at the current level); increments `consecutiveFailures` only after a real failed/error/timeout outcome. Both INFO heartbeat lines (the connect-attempt one and the already_in_flight one) gained ` | consecutive_failures=K | next_attempt_in_seconds=Z` suffixes; the 3 WARNING loud-bails now print `backoffSec` instead of the fixed `tickIntervalSec` in their `next attempt in %.1fs` text. No explicit counter reset: a successful connect (`outcome == 'success'`) returns from the loop, and the lifecycle daemon is re-spawned fresh on the next dropout -- so `tickIntervalSec` defaulting to HEARTBEAT_TICK_INTERVAL_SEC=10.0 means the next outage starts at the base interval again (no permanent slowdown; covered by test_successThenNewOutage_startsAtBaseInterval_noPermanentSlowdown). Docstring + mod-history header updated. HEARTBEAT_ATTEMPT_TIMEOUT_SEC (30.0, the per-attempt wall-clock cap) and the inFlightProbeFn / isConnectInFlight seam are untouched. NOTE: the story's doNotTouch line says HEARTBEAT_ATTEMPT_TIMEOUT_SEC 'becomes the BASE for backoff' -- that conflates two constants; per the explicit scope.filesToTouch text ('existing tickIntervalSec parameter becomes BASE') the BASE is the `tickIntervalSec` parameter (default HEARTBEAT_TICK_INTERVAL_SEC=10s), NOT the 30s attempt cap, which was left alone. The lifecycle.py callsite passes no tickIntervalSec, so production BASE = 10s -> ladder is 10,20,40,80,160,320s then flat at 320s (never the 900s ceiling at base 10 -- BASE*2^5=320<900); the 900s cap and BACKOFF_EXP_CAP clamp are still exercised by test_backoffCappedAtMaxBackoffSec (BASE=300) and test_consecutiveFailures_intervalsDoubleUntilExponentCap (8 ticks).)

tests/pi/obdii/test_reconnect_loop_backoff.py (NEW -- 12 tests / 4 classes. TestExponentialBackoffLadder x4: test_consecutiveFailures_intervalsDoubleUntilExponentCap (8 failed ticks @ BASE=10 -> sleeps [10,20,40,80,160,320,320,320]; FAILS pre-fix where the list is [10,10,10,10,10,10,10,10] -- the fixed-cadence discriminator), test_firstGapStaysAtBaseInterval_v0271BackCompat (sleeps == [10.0, 20.0]; first gap is BASE), test_backoffCappedAtMaxBackoffSec (BASE=300 -> [300,600,900,900,900]; max == MAX_BACKOFF_SEC), test_inFlightSkipDoesNotAdvanceBackoff (in-flight ticks sleep at the current level + don't bump the counter -> [10,10,10,20]). TestBackoffResetsOnSuccess x1: a flaky-then-up connectFn -> [10,20,40] then connect (no 4th sleep); a fresh call -> [10.0] (no permanent slowdown). TestHeartbeatLogLineFields x2: the INFO line carries consecutive_failures=0/1/2 + next_attempt_in_seconds=10.0/20.0/40.0 across 3 ticks (and still carries last_attempt_outcome=); the already_in_flight line carries the same new fields with consecutive_failures=0. TestBackoffConstantsAndSignature x3: MAX_BACKOFF_SEC==900.0 + BACKOFF_EXP_CAP==5 + HEARTBEAT_TICK_INTERVAL_SEC==10.0 (the imports FAIL pre-fix -- BACKOFF_EXP_CAP/MAX_BACKOFF_SEC don't exist, an ImportError at collection time); single-tick caller signature unchanged (no new required kwargs); shutdownEvent still aborts the loop. Uses a local FakeClock (advances `now` by each sleep arg) mirroring test_reconnect_loop_heartbeat.py's pattern.)

feedback.grounding: Pre-flight audit per acceptance #1 (rg `HEARTBEAT_ATTEMPT_TIMEOUT_SEC|runReconnectHeartbeat|tickIntervalSec` src/pi/obdii/): runReconnectHeartbeat lives in src/pi/obdii/reconnect_loop.py; the ONLY production callsite is src/pi/obdii/orchestrator/lifecycle.py::_spawnReconnectHeartbeatDaemon, which calls `runReconnectHeartbeat(connectFn=, isConnectedFn=, inFlightProbeFn=, shutdownEvent=)` -- it passes NO `tickIntervalSec`, so production BASE = the default HEARTBEAT_TICK_INTERVAL_SEC = 10.0 (stopCondition #1 -- multiple callsites with different cadence expectations -- did NOT fire; the only other invokers are tests). The actual heartbeat *interval* is HEARTBEAT_TICK_INTERVAL_SEC (10s), not HEARTBEAT_ATTEMPT_TIMEOUT_SEC (30s) as I-025's prose says (HEARTBEAT_ATTEMPT_TIMEOUT_SEC is the per-connect wall-clock cap inside _attemptConnectWithCap) -- I followed the explicit scope text 'existing tickIntervalSec parameter becomes BASE'. stopCondition #2 (backoff conflicts with the V0.27.1 US-301 boot canary timing): _verifyReconnectDaemonAlive fires SYNCHRONOUSLY right after the daemon is spawned, before the heartbeat loop's first sleep, so the backoff (which only affects the inter-attempt sleeps) cannot move the canary out of its window -- did NOT fire. Design choice that keeps three otherwise-out-of-scope tests GREEN: `_nextAttemptInterval` is called with `consecutiveFailures` taken BEFORE the current tick is processed, so the first failed-tick sleep == BASE (exponent 0). That preserves (a) tests/pi/integration/test_engine_on_late_adapter_e2e.py's `sleepFn.sleepDurations == [10.0]` (tick1 fails -> sleep BASE, tick2 connects), (b) tests/pi/obdii/test_connect_thread_safety.py (sleepFn is a no-op there anyway, ticks/connectCalls unaffected), (c) tests/pi/integration/test_engine_drive_capture_e2e.py. The ONE existing test that the cadence change legitimately broke -- test_reconnect_loop_heartbeat.py::test_emitsThreeOrMoreHeartbeatsInThirtySeconds_perCioMandate's `clock.sleeps == [10.0]*4 or [10.0]*3` -- was updated (renamed + re-asserted to pin the per-tick-heartbeat US-301 invariant + the new [10,20,40,80] ladder); flagged in filesActuallyTouched as a scope-fence note since that file isn't in scope.filesToTouch.
